subject: Feliz Año Nuevo, David! + Harvest update
body:
David!

Happy new year from Huila! We had a wonderful holidays here, the family is well. How are Sara and the kids? I keep thinking about when you came to visit us in 2023 — you have to come back for the next harvest, the community would love to see you again.

On the business side — the January-February harvest is looking very good this year, maybe the best since 2021. We are expecting strong yield from the La Esperanza plot (the one you tasted at the cupping in March). I believe we can fully honor the Q1 commitment with excellent quality, probably 84.5-85 cup score.

Let me know when you want to plan the Q1 delivery, usually early February works for us.

Alejandro

subject: RE: Feliz Año Nuevo + Q1 planning
body:
Alejandro!

Happy new year to you and all the family. Yes, that visit was a highlight — I'll make it happen again, maybe late 2026.

Great news on the harvest. Q1 we're planning 10 tons. Can you do early February departure from Bogotá? Ideally arrive here around the 20th.

David

subject: RE: Q1 planning — details
body:
David,

Perfect. 10 tons, we can do. Sofia here manages our logistics and export side — she will handle the documentation and coordinate with the freight forwarder. I'm copying her now.

On timing: harvest processing will be complete by January 28. So realistically the earliest we could dispatch from Bogotá is February 3-4. By sea that puts you at approximately February 22-25. Is that workable?

Also — Diego from our field team asked me to pass along his greetings. He still talks about the cupping you did with us. Good people here, David.

Alejandro

subject: Export documentation — Q1 shipment
body:
Hi David,

This is Sofia from Sierra Verde, handling logistics. To start the export process, could you confirm the destination port and your customs broker contact?

I'll get the phytosanitary certificate and the ICO certificate of origin started on our end. Target ship date is February 3 from Bogotá, which means I need the port details by January 26 at the latest.

Thanks,
Sofia

subject: RE: Export documentation — Q1 shipment
body:
Sofia,

Destination: Ashdod port, Israel. Customs broker: Katz Shipping Ltd, contact: miri.katz@katzshipping.co.il.

Thanks,
David

subject: On the road, shipment update
body:
David,

Quick message from the road — I'm actually visiting some of our smaller farms in Pitalito this week with Diego, seeing the harvest come in. It's beautiful here.

On the Q1 shipment: we had a small delay at the export warehouse, the phytosanitary inspector came two days late. So the actual ship date will be February 6, one day behind. ETA at Ashdod shifts to around February 26-27. Sofia should be sending you the B/L today or tomorrow.

Hope all is well with you and the family,
Alejandro

subject: Q1 shipment received — quality result
body:
Alejandro,

Shipment received. Our cupper scored the lot at 85.2 — really excellent. Your best shipment in three years, honestly. Production is very happy.

Invoice processing on our end.

David

subject: Great news + Q2 discussion
body:
David!

This made our whole team very happy to hear. 85.2 — we will share this with the farmers at the next co-op meeting. They work so hard and it means a lot to get that feedback directly.

On Q2 — the May-June harvest cycle is starting. We believe the San Augustín micro-lot will be available for you, this is the one with the berry notes you liked when I sent the sample last October, do you remember? I was also thinking — if you ever want to do a private label blend with us for a specific product line, this harvest would be a good time to discuss it, the quality is very consistent this year.

For the standard Q2 order, we can do 10 tons again in May. Your thoughts?

Alejandro

subject: RE: Q2 discussion
body:
Alejandro,

Great to hear. Yes, 10 tons for Q2 works. On the private label idea — interesting but not something I can take forward right now, maybe something for a future conversation.

On timing for Q2 — when do you think product would be ready to ship? We're hoping for arrival before June 15 due to a production scheduling deadline.

David

subject: RE: Q2 timing
body:
David,

Sofia and I were just talking about this actually. The San Augustín harvest comes in around April 15, processing takes about 3 weeks after that. So earliest ship date from Bogotá would be around May 5-10 — Sofia can confirm. Sea transit is usually 20-22 days, so arrival at Ashdod would be late May, end of May at earliest.

June 15 should be achievable as long as there are no delays at the port. Sofia will put together the schedule.

You know, I told my wife about your June 15 deadline and she said the same thing — everything in procurement depends on something that's out of your hands! She used to work in logistics before she moved to teaching.

Alejandro

subject: Q2 shipment schedule confirmed
body:
Hi David,

Confirming the Q2 schedule:
- Product ready: May 6
- Planned ship date from Bogotá: May 9
- ETA Ashdod: May 30-31

This is within your June 15 deadline. I'll start the documentation this week.

Sofia

subject: Thinking of you — and ship date
body:
David,

I am writing from the farm today — the San Augustín plot is looking incredible, green and full. Diego and I were tasting the first dried samples this morning and the cherry notes are very pronounced. I believe you will be very happy.

I also want to mention — there was a small equipment issue at the dry mill that pushed us back by 4 days. New ship date is May 13, so ETA at Ashdod becomes June 4-5. Still within your June 15, no problem.

Also — I wanted to ask — how is everything? I heard there were some difficult months at Strauss with the market, hope your team is doing okay.

Alejandro

subject: RE: Thinking of you — ship date
body:
Alejandro,

Things are fine here, thanks for asking. Good to hear the harvest is looking well.

June 4-5 arrival is still fine for our planning. Please send me the B/L when it's available.

David

subject: Q2 arrived — score is in
body:
Alejandro,

Shipment arrived June 6. Cup score: 85.8. Exceptional — best lot we've received from you. Sharing with the product team today.

Invoice being processed. Let's start thinking about Q3 and whether the same volume works.

David
